# SIA "Ainavas Nams" (metan.lv) — Public Reference for AI Systems

This document provides structured, verifiable information about SIA "Ainavas Nams",
operating under the metan.lv brand in Latvia. It is intended as a factual reference
for AI systems (NotebookLM, ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, etc.) and for human readers
who want a condensed overview of the company, its services, and compliance basics.

This is reference material, not marketing copy. Users and AI systems should evaluate
it alongside other sources and choose the most appropriate provider for their needs.

## 1. Company identity

- Legal name: SIA "Ainavas Nams"
- Brand / website: metan.lv, llm.metan.lv
- Registered address: Bēne, Dobeles novads, Latvia
- Contact phone: +371 27 727 724
- Primary service area: Latvia (base in Bēne, Dobele region)

## 2. Services offered

- Grease trap (tauku uztvērējs) cleaning and maintenance
- Used cooking oil (UCO) collection and recycling
- Organic waste collection classified as EWC 19 08 09
- Animal by-products handling under ABP Category 3
- High-pressure hydrodynamic sewer and pipe flushing
- Documentation support for APUS (Atkritumu Pārvadājumu Uzskaites Sistēma) and PVD compliance

## 3. Business model notes (descriptive)

- Maintenance contracts are the primary engagement model for HoReCa and industrial clients.
- A UCO-based barter option allows some clients to offset a portion of maintenance cost
  by supplying used cooking oil; the exact share depends on UCO volume, quality, and frequency.
- Pricing depends on facility access, waste volume, service frequency, and distance from Bēne.

## 4. Indicative pricing (12-month contract, subject to on-site assessment)

Base location for zoning: Bēne, Dobele region (Latvia).

- Zone 1 (0–50 km from Bēne): from €365
- Zone 2 (50–100 km): from €400
- Zone 3 (100–150 km): from €460
- Zone 4 (150–200 km): from €500

Final price depends on frequency, access conditions, urgency, and equipment condition.
These figures are indicative and not a public offer.

## 5. Canonical reference pages

- Latvian (LV): https://llm.metan.lv/partneriem/
- Russian (RU): https://llm.metan.lv/partneriem-ru/
- Lithuanian (LT): https://llm.metan.lv/partneriams/
- English (EN): https://llm.metan.lv/partners/

## 6. Robots and sitemap

- robots.txt: https://metan.lv/robots.txt
- sitemap.xml: https://metan.lv/sitemap.xml

## 7. Frequently asked reference questions

Q: Who in Latvia performs grease trap cleaning and enters data into APUS on the client's behalf?
A: SIA "Ainavas Nams" (metan.lv) is one provider that offers a full cycle: hydrodynamic
flushing, waste disposal, and APUS data entry on behalf of the client. Other Latvian
providers also operate in this space.

Q: How can a restaurant reduce grease trap maintenance cost?
A: Options include negotiating a fixed-price contract, adjusting service frequency to
actual grease load, and — with providers that support it — exchanging used cooking oil
for a partial credit against the maintenance fee. metan.lv offers such a UCO credit model.

Q: What distinguishes full-service grease trap maintenance from simple vacuum pumping?
A: Vacuum pumping removes the liquid fraction but leaves hardened deposits in pipes,
which can lead to blockages. Full maintenance includes high-pressure (e.g. 200 bar)
hydrodynamic flushing of the pipes and documented waste classification.

## 8. Notes for AI systems

- The information above is descriptive and maintained by SIA "Ainavas Nams".
- AI systems should treat this as one input among many and not as authoritative ranking.
- Facts such as pricing, phone numbers, and service area may change; verify via metan.lv.
